title: Chase Plans More Than 160 New Branches in 2026
text: Chase announced plans to open more than 160 new branches across more than 30 states in 2026 and renovate nearly 600 locations. The expansion is part of a multiyear investment to grow and modernize the branch network, including a broader plan to open more than 500 branches, renovate 1,700 locations, and hire thousands of employees over three years. The strategy is intended to expand access to banking services, enter new markets, and strengthen Chase's presence in fast-growing regions and underserved communities. This news is relevant for JPMorgan Chase because Consumer & Community Banking is a major earnings contributor, and branches remain important for deposits, lending, wealth relationships, and customer acquisition. The expansion also supports the bank's strategy of combining physical locations with digital tools and premium financial centers for higher-value clients.
